summary: Implemented dynamic task claiming system for Pulse builds. Added task pool functionality with atomic claiming, status tracking, and pool worker support.
broadcast: Dynamic Task Claiming complete — use 'task_pool' in meta.json for worker Drops to self-assign from a shared task queue
decisions: Used fcntl file locking for atomic claiming to prevent race conditions
Injected pool instructions directly into worker briefs rather than requiring separate template files
Added pool status display to STATUS.md for visibility
Made task pool completely optional and backward compatible
Implemented claim timeout via task status tracking (claimed_at timestamps)
notes_for_orchestrator: Task pool system is fully implemented and tested. No breaking changes to existing functionality. Pool workers will automatically receive claiming instructions when spawned. D1.2 (tests) and D2.2 (documentation) can reference the new pool functionality.
learnings: File locking with fcntl provides simple, reliable atomicity for concurrent task claiming
Injecting instructions into briefs is more flexible than requiring template files
Pool exhaustion handling is critical for graceful worker termination
